name: Russian Cuisine
description: Borscht, beef stroganoff, vodka, and the dishes of the Russian table.

questions.q: What vegetable gives borscht its signature deep red color?

source.notes: Beetroot is also the answer to several other questions in this category.

questions.q: What's the Russian name for sour cream, often used as a garnish?

source.notes: Spooned onto soups, dumplings, and pancakes.

questions.q: Beef Stroganoff — sautéed beef in sour-cream sauce — was invented in which century?

source.notes: Named for the wealthy Stroganov family of mid-19th-century Russia.

questions.q: What is shchi?

source.notes: One of Russia's oldest national dishes; documented since the 9th century.

questions.q: What is the main protein in caviar that Russia is famous for producing?

source.notes: The classic Russian caviar — Beluga, Ossetra, Sevruga — all comes from sturgeon.

questions.q: Which is NOT one of the three classic types of Caspian sturgeon caviar?

source.notes: Beluga, Ossetra, and Sevruga are all Caspian sturgeon species.

questions.q: What are syrniki?
What are pelmeni?

source.notes: Small dumplings filled with meat, traditionally served with smetana.

questions.q: What is shashlik?

source.notes: Russian/Caucasian skewered grilled meat — analogous to a kebab.

questions.q: What savory pie filling is featured in the elaborate Russian classic 'coulibiac'?

source.notes: Hot fish-and-rice pie often featuring salmon, eggs, and dill.

questions.q: What is mors — a traditional Russian beverage?

source.notes: Made from cranberries, lingonberries, or other northern berries.

questions.q: What is kvass — a popular Russian beverage with a fermented bread base?
choices: A lightly alcoholic fermented bread drink

source.notes: Around 1.2% ABV — sold from street tanks in summer.

questions.q: Which Russian Tsar is credited with popularizing coffee in 18th-century Russia?

source.notes: Encountered coffee in Holland and brought the habit home.

questions.q: Which Russian distiller's vodka brand became globally iconic after his work in the 19th century?

source.notes: Pyotr Arsenjevich Smirnov began producing vodka in 1860s Moscow.

questions.q: In what year did the first McDonald's open in Moscow, Russia?

source.notes: January 31, 1990, in Pushkin Square — at the time the busiest McDonald's in the world. Closed and rebranded after Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

questions.q: What is the legal drinking age in Russia?
What is solyanka?

source.notes: A thick, sour-spicy soup with mixed meats and pickles.

questions.q: What is pirozhki?

source.notes: Common fillings: meat, potato, cabbage, mushroom, sweet fruit.

questions.q: What are the famous yeasted Russian thin pancakes (similar to French crêpes), traditionally served at Maslenitsa?

source.notes: Often topped with sour cream, caviar, or jam.

questions.q: What is okroshka?
choices: A cold soup based on kvass or kefir with chopped vegetables

source.notes: Beloved as a refreshing summer dish.
